name: up-bank-api

metadata.name: up-bank-api
metadata.summary: 💎 Typed python client for Up's banking API

documentation:
# Up Bank API

[![Project version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/up-bank-api?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/up-bank-api)
[![Supported python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/up-bank-api?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/up-bank-api)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/jcwillox/up-bank-api?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/jcwillox/up-bank-api/blob/main/LICENSE)
[![CodeFactor Grade](https://img.shields.io/codefactor/grade/github/jcwillox/up-bank-api?style=flat-square)](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/jcwillox/up-bank-api)
[![Monthly Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/up-bank-api?style=flat-square)](https://pypistats.org/packages/up-bank-api)
[![Total Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pepy/dt/up-bank-api?style=flat-square)](https://pepy.tech/project/up-bank-api)
[![Code style](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/psf/black)

This is an unofficial Python API wrapper for the Australian Bank [Up's](https://up.com.au/) API.

This package is fully typed, documented, and it has an intuitive structure, so I'd recommend just having quick look at the [docs](https://jcwillox.github.io/up-bank-api), and letting syntax completion take the wheel.

- 📚 [Package Documentation](https://jcwillox.github.io/up-bank-api)
- 🕶️ [The Up Website](https://up.com.au)
- 📖 [Up API Documentation](https://developer.up.com.au)
- 🔗 [Up API on GitHub](https://github.com/up-banking/api)

## Installation

```shell
pip install up-bank-api
```

To include async support

```shell
pip install up-bank-api[async]
```

## Usage

To use this library you will need a personal access token which can be retrieved from https://developer.up.com.au. When using this library you can either provide the token directly or use the environment variable `UP_TOKEN`.

**Synchronous Client**

```python
from upbankapi import Client, NotAuthorizedException

# implicitly use the environment variable UP_TOKEN
client = Client()

# or directly provide token
client = Client(token="MY_TOKEN")

# check the token is valid
try:
    user_id = client.ping()
    print(f"Authorized: {user_id}")
except NotAuthorizedException:
    print("The token is invalid")
```

**Asynchronous Client**

```python
import asyncio
from upbankapi import AsyncClient, NotAuthorizedException

async def main():
    # implicitly use the environment variable UP_TOKEN
    client = AsyncClient()

    # or directly provide token
    client = AsyncClient(token="MY_TOKEN")

    # use a context manager to automatically close the aiohttp session
    async with AsyncClient() as client:
        try:
            user_id = await client.ping()
            print(f"Authorized: {user_id}")
        except NotAuthorizedException:
            print("The token is invalid")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
```

See the [docs](https://jcwillox.github.io/up-bank-api) for more information, examples and code reference.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.common.ModelBase._raw_response

The raw response data returned from the API.

This should **not** be used, it is only available for intermediary use to access properties
that have not yet been exposed or to view the entire response.
Base class for all models.

classes.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction

methods.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.pending
documentation: Returns true when the transaction is pending.

methods.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.long_description
documentation: Returns a longer description using the description and message properties.

methods.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.categorize
documentation:
Assign a category to this transaction.

Arguments:
    category: The category to assign to the transaction.
              Setting this to `None` will de-categorize the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the transaction is not `categorizable`.

methods.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.add_tags
documentation:
Add tags to this transaction.

Arguments:
    *tags: The tags or tag ids to add to the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

methods.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.remove_tags
documentation:
Remove tags from this transaction.

Arguments:
    *tags: The tags or tag ids to remove to the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

methods.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.__repr__
documentation: Return the representation of the transaction.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.id
documentation: The unique identifier for this transaction.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.status
documentation: The current processing status of this transaction.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.raw_text
documentation: The original, unprocessed text of the transaction.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.description
documentation: A short description for this transaction. Usually the merchant name for purchases.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.message
documentation: Attached message for this transaction, such as a payment message, or a transfer note.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.categorizable
documentation: Boolean flag set to `true` on transactions that support the use of categories.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.hold_info
documentation: The amount and foreign_amount of this transaction while it was/is in the HELD state.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.round_up
documentation:
Details of how this transaction was rounded-up.

If no Round Up was applied this field will be null.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.cashback
documentation:
Provides details of the reimbursement if all or part of 
this transaction was instantly reimbursed in the form of cashback.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.amount
documentation:
The amount of this transaction in Australian dollars.

For transactions that were once `HELD` but are now `SETTLED`, refer to the
`hold_info` field for the original amount the transaction was `HELD` at.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.amount_in_base_units
documentation: The amount of money in the smallest denomination for the currency.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.currency
documentation: The ISO 4217 currency code.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.foreign_amount
documentation:
The foreign currency amount of this transaction.

This field will be `None` for domestic transactions.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.card_purchase_method
documentation: Information about the card used for this transaction, if applicable.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.settled_at
documentation:
The `datetime` at which this transaction settled.

This field will be `None` for transactions that are currently in the `HELD` status.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.created_at
documentation: The `datetime` at which this transaction was first encountered.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.category
documentation: The category assigned to this transaction.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.transactions.Transaction.tags
documentation: The list of tags assigned to this transaction.
Representation of a Transaction.

modules.name: upbankapi.models
documentation: Models used for deserializing the data from the API.

classes.name: upbankapi.models.accounts.Account

methods.name: upbankapi.models.accounts.Account.transactions
documentation:
Retrieves transactions for this account.

Arguments:
    status: The transaction status for which to return records.
    since: The start `datetime` from which to return records.
    until: The end `datetime` up to which to return records.
    category: The category/id identifier for which to filter transactions.
              Raises exception for invalid category.
    tag: A transaction tag/id to filter for which to return records.
         Returns empty if tag does not exist.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the transactions.

methods.name: upbankapi.models.accounts.Account.__repr__
documentation: Return the representation of the account.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.accounts.Account.ownership_type

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.accounts.Account.balance

classes.name: upbankapi.models.categories.Tag

methods.name: upbankapi.models.categories.Tag.transactions
documentation:
Retrieves transactions for a specific account or all accounts.

Arguments:
    account: An account/id to fetch transactions from.
             If `None`, returns transactions across all accounts.
    status: The transaction status for which to return records.
    since: The start `datetime` from which to return records.
    until: The end `datetime` up to which to return records.
    category: The category/id identifier for which to filter transactions.
              Raises exception for invalid category.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the transactions.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.models.categories.Tag.id
documentation: The label of the tag, which also acts as the tags unique identifier.
Representation of a Tag.

Retrieves transactions for a specific account or all accounts.

    status: The transaction status for which to return records.
    since: The start `datetime` from which to return records.

    category: The category/id identifier for which to filter transactions.

Returns:
    A paginated list of the transactions.

Base class for all Up specific exceptions,
this will not cover all possible exceptions such as network or JSON decoding errors.

documentation: Raised for an invalid Up token.

classes.name: upbankapi.exceptions.RateLimitExceededException

classes.name: upbankapi.exceptions.BadResponseException

modules.name: upbankapi
documentation: Typed python client for interacting with Up's banking API.

classes.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.ping
documentation:
Retrieves the users unique id and checks if the token is valid.

Returns:
    The users unique id.

Raises:
    NotAuthorizedException: If the token is invalid.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.account
documentation:
Retrieve a single account by its unique account id.

Arguments:
    account_id: The unique identifier for an account.

Returns:
    The specified account.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.accounts
documentation:
Retrieves a list of the users accounts.

Arguments:
    type: The type of account for which to return records.
    ownership_type: The account ownership structure for which to return records.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the accounts.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.category
documentation:
Retrieve a category by its unique category id.

Arguments:
    category_id: The unique identifier for a category.

Returns:
    The specified category.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.categories
documentation:
Retrieves a list of categories.

Arguments:
    parent: The parent category/id to filter categories by.
            Raises exception for invalid category.

Returns:
    A list of the categories.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.categorize
documentation:
Assign a category to a transaction.

Arguments:
    transaction: The transaction/id to change the category on.
                 The transaction must be categorizable otherwise
                 a `ValueError` will be raised.
    category: The category to assign to the transaction.
              Setting this to `None` will de-categorize the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the transaction is not `categorizable`.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.tags
documentation:
Retrieves a list of the users tags.

Arguments:
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the tags.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.add_tags
documentation:
Add tags to a given transaction.

Arguments:
    transaction: The transaction/id to add tags on.
    *tags: The tags or tag ids to add to the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.remove_tags
documentation:
Remove tags from a given transaction.

Arguments:
    transaction: The transaction/id to remove tags on.
    *tags: The tags or tag ids to remove to the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.transaction
documentation:
Retrieve a single transaction by its unique id.

Arguments:
    transaction_id: The unique identifier for a transaction.

Returns:
    The specified transaction.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.transactions
documentation:
Retrieves transactions for a specific account or all accounts.

Arguments:
    account: An account/id to fetch transactions from.
             If `None`, returns transactions across all accounts.
    status: The transaction status for which to return records.
    since: The start `datetime` from which to return records.
    until: The end `datetime` up to which to return records.
    category: The category/id identifier for which to filter transactions.
              Raises exception for invalid category.
    tag: A transaction tag/id to filter for which to return records.
         Returns empty if tag does not exist.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the transactions.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncClient.webhooks
documentation:
Retrieves a list of the users webhooks.

Arguments:
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the webhooks.

Retrieve a single webhook by its unique id.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncWebhookAdapter.create
documentation:
Registers and returns a new webhook.

Arguments:
    url: The URL that this webhook should post events to.
    description: An optional description for this webhook, up to 64 characters in length.

Returns:
    The newly created webhook.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncWebhookAdapter.ping
documentation:
Pings a webhook by its unique id.

Arguments:

Returns:

documentation:
Retrieves the logs from a webhook by id.

Arguments:
    webhook: The webhook or webhook id to fetch logs from.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the webhook logs.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._async.AsyncWebhookAdapter.delete
documentation:
Deletes a webhook by its unique id.

Arguments:
    webhook: The webhook or webhook id to delete.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

    ownership_type: The account ownership structure for which to return records.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the accounts.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.category
documentation:
Retrieve a category by its unique category id.

Arguments:
    category_id: The unique identifier for a category.

Returns:
    The specified category.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.categories
documentation:
Retrieves a list of categories.

Arguments:
    parent: The parent category/id to filter categories by.
            Raises exception for invalid category.

Returns:
    A list of the categories.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.categorize
documentation:
Assign a category to a transaction.

Arguments:
    transaction: The transaction/id to change the category on.
                 The transaction must be categorizable otherwise
                 a `ValueError` will be raised.
    category: The category to assign to the transaction.
              Setting this to `None` will de-categorize the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the transaction is not `categorizable`.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.tags
documentation:
Retrieves a list of the users tags.

Arguments:
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the tags.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.add_tags
documentation:
Add tags to a given transaction.

Arguments:
    transaction: The transaction/id to add tags on.
    *tags: The tags or tag ids to add to the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.remove_tags
documentation:
Remove tags from a given transaction.

Arguments:
    transaction: The transaction/id to remove tags on.
    *tags: The tags or tag ids to remove to the transaction.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.transaction
documentation:
Retrieve a single transaction by its unique id.

Arguments:
    transaction_id: The unique identifier for a transaction.

Returns:
    The specified transaction.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.transactions
documentation:
Retrieves transactions for a specific account or all accounts.

Arguments:
    account: An account/id to fetch transactions from.
             If `None`, returns transactions across all accounts.
    status: The transaction status for which to return records.
    since: The start `datetime` from which to return records.
    until: The end `datetime` up to which to return records.
    category: The category/id identifier for which to filter transactions.
              Raises exception for invalid category.
    tag: A transaction tag/id to filter for which to return records.
         Returns empty if tag does not exist.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the transactions.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.webhooks
documentation:
Retrieves a list of the users webhooks.

Arguments:
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the webhooks.

class_variables.name: upbankapi.client._sync.Client.rate_limit
documentation: The information regarding the current rate limiting status.
Synchronous client for interacting with Up's API

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.WebhookAdapter.__call__
documentation:
Retrieve a single webhook by its unique id.

Arguments:
    webhook_id: The unique identifier for a webhook.

Returns:
    The specified webhook.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.WebhookAdapter.get
documentation:
Retrieve a single webhook by its unique id.

Arguments:
    webhook_id: The unique identifier for a webhook.

Returns:
    The specified webhook.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.WebhookAdapter.create
documentation:
Registers and returns a new webhook.

Arguments:
    url: The URL that this webhook should post events to.
    description: An optional description for this webhook, up to 64 characters in length.

Returns:
    The newly created webhook.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.WebhookAdapter.ping
documentation:
Pings a webhook by its unique id.

Arguments:
    webhook: The webhook or webhook id to ping.

Returns:
    The ping event response.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.WebhookAdapter.logs
documentation:
Retrieves the logs from a webhook by id.

Arguments:
    webhook: The webhook or webhook id to fetch logs from.
    limit: The maximum number of records to return.
    page_size: The number of records to return in each page. (max appears to be 100)

Returns:
    A paginated list of the webhook logs.

methods.name: upbankapi.client._sync.WebhookAdapter.delete
documentation:
Deletes a webhook by its unique id.

Arguments:
    webhook: The webhook or webhook id to delete.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, otherwise raises exception.
